description: A funnel/conversion chart for staged pipeline drop-off: an ORDERED stage dimension on rows ([none:Stage:nk], data/ordinal order preserved — deliberately NO computed sort) and the amount measure as a Bar on cols ([sum:Amount:qk]), also on the size encoding so the bars taper toward the narrow end of the funnel. Reading top-to-bottom the shrinking bars show stage-to-stage conversion / drop-off. Serves UC6 (pipeline health & deal flow). Tableau has no native funnel mark, so this is authored as creative Bar usage generalized from the proven ranking-ordered-bar structure (NO usable funnel exemplar exists in the twb corpus — grep 'funnel' returns nothing). Live render-verified 2026-07-06 by the golden-parity gate (composite 98; see portability_evidence.render_evidence): structural 0.97297 (5/5 critical), pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 4=4 Copy>Data mark counts and the positive no-captions caption-leak pass.
avoid_when: The stages have no inherent sequence and you want members ranked by value — use ranking-ordered-bar (which sorts DESC by the measure); a funnel encodes a fixed conversion ORDER, not a value ranking.
You need proportional multi-path flow between many source/target nodes — that is a sankey, not a single-path funnel.

The amount is not monotonically non-increasing across the stages (a later stage can carry more than an earlier one) — the funnel silhouette assumes drop-off and inverts when a stage rises (see hazard stage-skipping).

slots.notes: The ORDERED pipeline-stage dimension on rows — one funnel band per stage. The template applies NO computed sort, so stages render in the bound field's member order; bind an ordinal/ordered stage field (or set a manual member sort) so the conversion sequence is correct. See hazard stage-order-dependence. XML column-instance [none:Stage:nk].

The stage amount (deal value / count) — SUM([Amount]) → [sum:Amount:qk], the SAME instance on cols (bar length) AND on the size encoding (bar taper). One derivation, so no qualified key. replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Amount] in both places on bind.

hazards.detail: The template plots stages in the BOUND dimension's native member order (there is deliberately NO computed-sort element — sorting DESC by amount would reorder stages and destroy the sequence). A funnel's whole story is the fixed stage order (Lead → Qualified → Proposal → Closed); binding an unordered nominal stage field yields an arbitrary, meaningless funnel. Bind an ordinal/ordered stage field or set a manual member sort before trusting the silhouette.

Deals that skip stages, or are counted at more than one stage, break the monotonic drop-off the funnel assumes: a later stage can exceed an earlier one, so the silhouette inverts and the implied conversion rates mislead. The template cannot detect non-monotonic stages — validate the data is a clean single-count-per-stage funnel (or model conversion explicitly) before reading drop-off from the bars.

Tableau has no native funnel mark; this is an ordered Bar with amount on both length and size (taper) — a left-aligned conversion bar, NOT a centered/symmetric funnel. A true mirrored funnel needs a dual-axis with a negated companion measure, which the {stage, amount} slot vocabulary does not express. The chart communicates stage drop-off honestly but is not a geometrically symmetric funnel; do not claim symmetry.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) - has bitten someone: a pipeline amount summed across rows with a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP ...) adds different currency units as one number, so each stage bar and the implied conversion are silently WRONG. SUM([Amount]) feeds BOTH the bar length (cols) and the taper (size) with no currency awareness. Convert to a single reporting currency, or filter to one currency_code, before binding a monetary pipeline measure.

Compiled HERMETICALLY via the factory route (no live Tableau Desktop) and SUBSEQUENTLY live render-verified on 2026-07-06: portability_evidence.render_verified='live-2026-07-06' and fast_path_eligible=true. The golden-parity gate earned the stamp (composite 98: structural 0.97297, pixel 1.0, binding-sanity sane via reconciled 4=4 Copy>Data mark counts + the positive no-captions caption-leak pass) — the live render-readback proof the hermetic compile alone could not supply. See portability_evidence.render_evidence for the auditable basis.
